metadata.description: Industry Knowledge (Issue #36, Architectural Decision #5): reusable organizational learning, separate from engagement-specific AI Suggestions. Always considers implementation date, audit period, historical applicability, and future applicability — never recommends retroactive changes.
metadata.source: Simulated — Release 2 populates this from cross-engagement AI learning; Release 1 seeds representative entries.

items.title: Firewall vendor changed
items.description: A client migrated its perimeter firewall vendor. Rule sets, logging formats, and change-management evidence templates typically change with the new platform.
potentialControlUpdates: Network perimeter control descriptions referencing the prior vendor's rule syntax
potentialEvidenceUpdates: Change-ticket evidence template for network changes
potentialTestingUpdates: Sample selection should draw from post-migration change tickets only
potentialReportUpdates: System description language naming the firewall vendor
items.historicalApplicability: Does not apply to testing performed before 2026-03-01 — the prior vendor's evidence remains valid for that period.
items.futureApplicability: Applies to all testing and evidence collected on or after 2026-03-01.

items.title: Cloud IAM provider migrated to short-lived credentials
items.description: A client's cloud-hosted product line moved from long-lived service account keys to short-lived, automatically rotated credentials. Evidence of key rotation and access review cadence typically changes with this shift.
potentialControlUpdates: Service account key rotation control language
potentialEvidenceUpdates: IAM configuration export should show credential TTL, not key age
potentialTestingUpdates: Testing procedures referencing key rotation cadence need updating to reference credential TTL policy
items.historicalApplicability: Does not apply to testing performed before 2026-05-15.
items.futureApplicability: Applies to all IAM-related testing and evidence collected on or after 2026-05-15 for cloud-hosted systems.
